title: RESIDENT EVIL 2 / BIOHAZARD RE:2

notes:
The only tweak needed for the game to work was to set there WINEPREFIX to the location of the game, install "mf" from winetricks and download this script "https://github.com/tonix64/python-installcab" then edit the file "installcab.py" and uncomment lines 129-137 and then run "./install-mf-64.sh" (If the user is using ARCH based OS like manjaro chances are they will need to edit the top line of installcab.py to use python2 or the script will fail) when it's finished, get the Microsoft "mfplat.dll" version 12.0.7601.23471 and paste it in the games exe directory. That's all. The game basically needs "Microsoft Media Foundation" to play the video clips. Make sure you mention the user needs to get the mfplat.dll from a windows 7 installation they own and that same version.

Performance is perfect quick video can be seen here of my demonstration. Completed the game twice already. 
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JCPgIyZTHA0
title: RESIDENT EVIL 2 / BIOHAZARD RE:2

notes:
This game runs perfectly on my system after updating DXVK by dragging updated DLLs into the game directory, and obtaining mfplat.dll from Windows 7 and placing that in the game directory too. I used the utility python-installcab on my proton prefix but some have reported this is not necessary. I have played for 34 hours with only 2 crashes as of writing. These crashes are not proton/linux exclusive, I've seen other people experience rare crashes on Windows as well.

Performance is great, but I have no comparison as I've never played it on Windows. 
The only issue I have discovered is that the mouse controls do not work properly by default. You could probably fix this, but it's abundantly clear this game is *not* made to be played on a keyboard and mouse either way, and I highly recommend against doing so.
title: RESIDENT EVIL 2 / BIOHAZARD RE:2

notes: Playthrough of Leon's campaign (and 2/3 of Clair's). Game runs perfectly, after some tweaks. In short: Update Proton to DXVK 0.96 and install mf (Media Foundation). There's a guide on YouTube: https://youtu.be/pcCJSm3IoMY After those tweaks, I didn't encounter a single issue and had rather solid 60fps on 1080p with high settings. Here's a snippet: https://youtu.be/husGux0CcV8
title: RESIDENT EVIL 2 / BIOHAZARD RE:2

notes:
I finished both campaigns without a single crash after the initial setup! 

I was playing on recommended graphics settings and framerate was pretty jumpy ranging from (perceived) 60 fps to 30 fps. But overall a very pleasant and playable experience. Then again my machine is pretty mid-range nowadays.

Controller worked, Graphics worked, Audio worked, Everything worked like a native experience!

I had to apply the already mentioned tweaks (drop .dlls from DXVK 0.96, mfplat.dll into the main folder and install the needed winetricks) 

Guide video here:
https://youtu.be/pcCJSm3IoMY
mfplat.dll from here: 
https://www.reddit.com/r/SteamPlay/comments/amde9g/resident_evil_2_is_finishable_in_linux/efmu1il

And after that 10 minute setup I was playing RE2 a week after launch from beginning to end on linux. Amazing. Thanks to everybody related to the Proton project!

notes:

title: RESIDENT EVIL 2 / BIOHAZARD RE:2

notes: Granted playtime has been minimal and might run into a crash or two later.  However the short time spent has been very playable.  Slight graphics flickering and input 'rubber banding" but hasn't been a deal breaker.  Was initially frustrated.  Had followed all the steps mentioned in other users posts.  Found out I was using the wrong mfplat.dll, which caused a black screen startup crash.  Re-reading a reddit post linked in a report below, I found out the correct mfplat.dll should be 430.6kb in size, and the rest is history.
title: RESIDENT EVIL 2 / BIOHAZARD RE:2

notes: Runs better than I expected.  Granted 15 minutes is not a lot of playtime.  Graphic settings pushed as far as a 6gb card will allow at 1080p.  Slight input weirdness at times, a few graphic anomalies here and there, but otherwise a smooth experience.  Spent more time than I want to admit getting this to run.  The correct mfplat.dll version is critical.  Re-reading one of the reddit posts here a day later, I found I was using the wrong version.  The correct one is 430kb in size.  Kudos to the pioneers who provided youtube videos and reddit links.
title: RESIDENT EVIL 2 / BIOHAZARD RE:2

notes:
With the mfplat workaround mentioned in other reports, the game runs stable, has no graphical issues with either RADV or the -PRO driver (amdvlk is borked), and performance is solid with latest DXVK, with frame rates being well above 60 at all times at 1080p with slightly toned down settings.

Mouse input is broken to the point that the game becomes unplayable, the cursor erratically jumps over the screen in the menu and a similar thing happens with the camera. Mouse buttons would lock up in "clicked" state until clicked again. This is a major drawback and the reason I'm rating it Bronze rather than Silver.

Wineserver load is high despite having Esync enabled, so weak CPUs may suffer.

Xbox One controller worked right out of the box.

title: RESIDENT EVIL 2 / BIOHAZARD RE:2

notes:
I disabled steam overlay. Used latest kernel and mesa. I also used DXVK 1.0 by dropping the .dlls directly into the games folder.

Lastly to fix the mfplat issue (game sits on loading forever) I did this:

Installed winetricks mf. So just "sudo apt install winetricks" then run "winetricks mf"

Download scripts from https://github.com/tonix64/python-installcab

Edit installcab.py the way described here: tonix64/python-installcab#1
(uncomment lines 129-137 which look like value = value.replace("%SystemRoot%", "C:\\windows"))

Launch the terminal in the folder with these scripts and run this:
$ export WINEPREFIX=/your_path_to_steam_library/steamapps/compatdata/883710/pfx/

$ ./install-mf-64.sh

Copy mfplat.dll to /your_path_to_steam_library/steamapps/common/RESIDENT EVIL 2 BIOHAZARD RE2/

NOTE mfplat.dll needs to be the windows 7 420kb version. It can be downloaded. Or acquired through a windows vm. Downloading it is far easier tbh.

title: RESIDENT EVIL 2 / BIOHAZARD RE:2

notes:
To get the game to run after the introduction cut scene and gas station gameplay sequence, you do the following:

Install Winetricks

"sudo apt install winetricks" 

then run 

"winetricks mf"

While that is running download the Python-installcab scripts from the github at:
https://github.com/tonix64/python-installcab

Edit "installcab.py"

uncomment the lines 129-137 starting with:

"value = value.replace("%SystemRoot%", "C:\\windows")"
ending with:

"value = value.replace("%ProgramData%", "C:\\ProgramData")"


Once the "winetricks mf" command has finished running, open a Terminal in the folder you saved the Python-installcab Python scripts into:

export WINEPREFIX="/path to steam library/steamapps/compatdata/883710/pfx/"

In my case this was:

export WINEPREFIX="/mnt/853f46c9-21fe-48d3-b915-1177385a6eb4/Games/Steam Games/steamapps/compatdata/883710/pfx/"


Next run the following:

./install-mf-64.sh

Download the mfplat.dll file from the following website and copy to your game folder

https://www.dllme.com/getfile.php?file=1008&id=54b5dcd55b223bc5df50b82e1e9e86b1

You want the Following mfplat.dll file
Size: 420.5KB
mfplat.dll
by Microsoft Corporation
Version: 12.0.7600.16385 (win7_rtm.0907131255)

title: RESIDENT EVIL 2 / BIOHAZARD RE:2

notes:
Game runs flawlessly, FPS was very smooth and my Xbox One controller worked great with the game (including rumble feedback). You will need to install the Media Foundation tweak to get cutscenes to work properly otherwise the game will crash anytime a cutscene is shown (basically this workaround is MANDATORY as you won't be able to make it very far into the game otherwise). Here's a step-by-step guide, it's really not too difficult:

Step 1. Download RE2: Remake in Steam (if you haven't already).
Step 2. Launch the game. When you get to the main menu, exit out completely (this step is important as it creates a specific directory in your system that we'll need later).
Step 3. Head over to https://github.com/z0z0z/mf-installcab and download the repository (click on the green button that says "Clone or Download" then choose "Download Zip").
Step 4. Extract the downloaded zip file and enter into the extracted folder. You should see a file called mfplat.dll, we'll come back to that one later. Also you should see another file called install-mf-64.sh, you'll want to enable executing that file as a program (in most distros you can just right-click the file, choose "Properties", choose "Permissions", then tick the box that states "Allow executing file as a program").
Step 5. While in the same folder, open up a terminal. Type the following command, substitute "user-name" with your actual user name: 

WINEPREFIX="/home/user-name/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/883710/pfx" ./install-mf-64.sh

If everything worked correctly, the terminal should do its thing and then state something about moving mfplat.dll into the game directory.

Step 6. Copy & paste the mfplat.dll file into the RE2 game directory, by default it should be located in /home/user-name/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Resident Evil 2 (note: you may need to enable "show hidden files" in your file manager in order to actually locate the game's directory, please refer to your specific distro's documentation for more info).

And that's it, the game should run fine after that!
title: RESIDENT EVIL 2 / BIOHAZARD RE:2
